name: Alachua County Sports and Events Center
description: Alachua County Sports and Events Center is a place to play pickleball in Gainesville, FL. There are 21 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. The courts cannot be reserved. The facility includes amenities like food, restrooms, water, lights, trainers or lessons, and wheelchair accessibility.
open_play_schedule: Schedule is based on all event booking. Please see RADDSports Pickleball on social media for up to date court schedule
fees: Fee required — At present $10 per player per session, but prices will be changing and discounts will be offered.

name: Albert "Ray" Massey Park
description: Come play pickleball at Albert "Ray" Massey Park in Gainesville, FL! There are 3 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are free. The courts can be reserved.

name: Brentwood School
description: Brentwood School is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Gainesville, FL. There is 1 outdoor concrete court. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The court is private.

name: Eastside High School
description: Eastside High School is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Gainesville, FL. There are 3 outdoor hard courts. You’ll have to create your own lines and bring your own net. The courts are free. Amenities include lights.

name: Flavet Field
description: Flavet Field is a place to play pickleball in Gainesville, FL. There are 4 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free.

name: Gainesville Y - North Central Florida YMCA
description: Members can use the courts for free, but non-members have to pay a fee when they want to use the courts.

name: Haile Plantation Golf and Country Club
description: Haile Plantation Golf and Country Club is a place to play pickleball in Gainesville, FL. There are 2 outdoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. A membership is required to play.

name: Hal Brady Recreation Complex
description: Come play pickleball at Hal Brady Recreation Complex in Alachua, FL! There are 6 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. Players have access to restrooms and water. The courts are free.

name: High Springs Civic Center
description: High Springs Civic Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in High Springs, FL. There are 4 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Jonesville Tennis Center
description: Jonesville Tennis Center is a place to play pickleball in Gainesville, FL. There are 3 outdoor clay courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are free. You can’t reserve the courts. Players have access to a pro shop or equipment store, restrooms, water, and wheelchair accessibility.

name: Joseph Williams Elementary
description: Come play pickleball at Joseph Williams Elementary in Gainesville, FL! There is 1 indoor asphalt court. You’ll have to create your own lines and bring your own net. The court is private.

name: Kanapaha Veterans Memorial Park
description: Come play pickleball at Kanapaha Veterans Memorial Park in Gainesville, FL! There are 5 outdoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are free.

name: Martin Luther King Center
description: Martin Luther King Center is a place to play pickleball in Gainesville, FL. There are 4 indoor wood courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Players have access to restrooms, water, wheelchair accessibility, and lights.

name: Northside Park
description:
4 permanent nets, 6 portable areas with permanent lines. No paddle system, wait at the court you want to play on. 

Bleachers are available for each of the 4 courts with permanent nets.

name: Saint Francis Catholic Academy
description: Courts are only open to currently enrolled students.

name: The 300 Club
description: Come play pickleball at The 300 Club in Gainesville, FL! There are 8 outdoor clay and hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like a pro shop or equipment store, restrooms, and water. You can reserve the courts. A membership is required to play.
fees: Members only — Guest Fees: $3 per visit or $25 Unlimited Monthly Fee

name: Weatherly HOA
description: This is our neighborhood tennis court that has been converted into pickleball. The gravel/asphalt is not great. There are lines and temporary nets for two pickleball courts. The nets are locked in a storage container and the code can be provided by contacting management or another Weatherly resident.
open_play_schedule: Please feel free to schedule your own games any time or drop in

name: Westside Baptist Church
description: Westside Baptist Church is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Gainesville, FL. There are 3 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. You can’t reserve the courts. The courts are free.
